_comment: Per-engine regression pins for CrispEmbed. Each entry pins (a) the GGUF under test to a specific HF revision SHA so an upstream re-quantise can't silently regress us, and (b) for diff-tier entries the frozen reference archive (also a pinned repo+path+revision). See tests/regression/README.md. Two tiers: `diff` runs test-<engine>-diff and asserts per-stage cos_min >= threshold; `smoke` runs the crispembed CLI and asserts the output is sane (or matches a captured golden) — the confirmation tier for engines without a ref yet. Re-run tools/audit_diff_coverage.py --probe-hf after editing.

progress._note: Kaggle kernels push the rolling progress.jsonl AND each completed engine's result record here after EVERY step, so a crashed/timed-out kernel still leaves every finished verdict on HF.

ref_convention: Reference archives live in each model's own HF GGUF repo under diff-harness-ref/<engine>-ref.gguf (qari-ocr already follows this). Legacy refs at the repo root (instructir-ref.gguf, hat-ref.gguf, pan-ref.gguf, granite-*-ref.gguf) are pinned as-is below.

backends.name: instructir
backends.diff_thresholds_note: Self-consistent torch ref; verified cos=1.000000 max_abs=6.6e-4 on f32 (PLAN.md).

backends.name: granite-vision
backends.diff_thresholds_note: q8_0 (q4_k floors near 0.99 per memory metal-mul-mm note). Harness loads both granite-vision-ref + granite-llm-ref from the same repo.

backends.name: hat
backends.diff_thresholds_note: Self-consistent torch ref; HAT harness built-in pass gate is 0.99.

backends.name: pan
backends.diff_thresholds_note: Verified cos_min=0.999997 vs self-consistent torch ref (PLAN.md).

backends.name: all-MiniLM-L6-v2

args: Hello world, this is a regression smoke test.

backends.smoke_note: Confirmation tier: no ref yet. Asserts the engine loads and emits a finite, correctly-shaped 384-d embedding. Promote to a diff entry once a per-layer ref is baked (tests/parity_layers_bert.py covers BERT layers).

args: Hello world, this is a regression smoke test.
query: regression smoke test for multilingual e5
